name: Tour Lugares Machadianos de Soria — circular
name_original: Ruta Lugares Machadianos de Soria — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Soria offers diverse landscapes, from the dramatic river canyons of Cañón del Río Lobos to the glacial lakes and high peaks of the Urbión Mountains. The province features extensive pine forests, river valleys, and rolling hills, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Trails lead through natural parks, past ancient sites, and along the Duero River, catering to different hiking preferences. This region in Castile and León is characterized by its natural heritage and varied elevations. Best hiking trails around Soria The most popular hiking route is Machadian Places of Soria Trail, an easy 1.8 miles (2.8 km) trail that takes about 47 minutes to complete. This route explores cultural and natural points of interest within Soria. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tour Lugares Machadianos de Soria — circular, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This circular trail offers varied scenery, including views of the Duero River and surrounding hills. Local hikers also love the Postiguillo Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through riverside areas, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Hiking around Soria is defined by river canyons, dense pine forests, and mountain peaks, including the Sierra del Moncayo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Soria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 480 hikers have used komoot to explore Soria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Soria?
A: Soria offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore everything from river canyons and dense pine forests to high mountain peaks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Soria?
A: Yes, Soria features 17 easy hiking trails perfect for families or those new to hiking. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056643664/machadian-places-of-soria-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Machadian Places of Soria Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer gentle walks exploring cultural and natural points of interest. The area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4709888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Muriel&lt;/a&gt; also provides an easy, round-trip option for a shorter hike.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on hikes in Soria?
A: Soria's hiking trails lead to a variety of stunning natural attractions. You can discover dramatic river canyons like the Cañón del Río Lobos, explore glacial lakes such as Laguna Negra in the Urbión Mountains, or visit picturesque waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Serena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also traverse extensive pine forests and offer panoramic views from peaks like Moncayo and Pico Urbión.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Soria?
A: Yes, Soria offers several circular hiking routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379166206/tour-lugares-machadianos-de-soria-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour Lugares Machadianos de Soria — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which provides varied scenery including views of the Duero River. The area around Covaleda also features circular routes to its waterfalls, and the Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión has circular options for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Soria?
A: The hiking trails in Soria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 45 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained routes that allow for exploration of the region's natural heritage.

Q: Are there any hikes that follow the Duero River?
A: Yes, the banks of the Duero River provide excellent hiking opportunities. Natural paths like the Path of the Duero follow its course, connecting villages and offering serene walks. Specific approved routes include PR SO 112 by Sierra Santa Ana and PR SO 113 Valhondo, which you can explore via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064121342/valhondo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valhondo Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails with significant elevation gain for a more challenging hike?
A: Absolutely. While there are many easy and moderate trails, Soria also offers 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking more significant elevation gain. Peaks like Pico Urbión and the illustrious San Miguel in the Sierra del Moncayo provide demanding hikes with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Soria's trails often blend nature with history. In the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, you can find the famous Big Cave next to the Hermitage of San Bartolomé. Near the city of Soria, Mount Valonsadero features ancient cave paintings. The town of Caracena, located in the southern part of the province, also boasts a castle and unique churches accessible via trails in the Canyon of Caracena.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Soria?
A: Soria offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year due to its diverse landscapes. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for exploring, with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is ideal for higher altitude trails, while some areas might be accessible for winter hikes, especially at lower elevations.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can hike to in Soria?
A: Yes, Soria is home to several beautiful waterfalls accessible by hiking. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Serena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; near Duruelo de la Sierra, or the Cascadas de Covaleda which are part of picturesque circular routes. Other notable waterfalls include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso Peñoncito Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails near the city of Soria itself?
A: Yes, there are several popular hiking options close to the city of Soria. Mount Valonsadero is a natural spot with multiple trekking routes through oak forests and grasslands. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379155541/ermita-de-san-saturio-y-cerro-de-santa-ana-circular-desde-casco-historico-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Saturio y Cerro de Santa Ana — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which starts from the historic center and offers views of the Duero River.
tips: The church of Santo Domingo, from the 12th century, is one of the jewels of Soria's Romanesque. Especially noteworthy is its cover rich in stone carvings.

In the center of the square are the coats of arms of the "Twelve Houses of Soria", noble families who have had a significant influence on the development of the city. Twelve Lineages of Soria is nowadays a Spanish noble institution of the descendants of the ancient families and maintains charitable and social activities for the benefit of the city
In the evening hours, Soria's main square with its historic buildings has a very special charm. Best enjoyed in one of the street cafes.

This park is the quintessential viewpoint of the city. The point that crowns it and delights the visitor with the best panoramic views, monumental views, the curve with which the Duero River embraces the city, the mountains…..and much more.
Old medieval bridge built in stone and that allows us to cross the Duero river. A monument of the city of Soria.

The trail along the Duero River in Soria offers several spectacular routes that combine nature, history, and culture.
Valhondo Route (PR-SO 113)
Route through the Sierra de Santa Ana (PR-SO 112)
Route of the Bridges of the Duero River (Covaleda)
Soria - Garray and the Duero River Banks Route

Tradition has it that in the 6th century the nobleman Saturio from Soria, after the death of his parents, distributed his wealth among the poor and went to live in some caves next to the Duero. There, in a sober and mystical oratory, in honor of Saint Michael the Archangel, he would live for thirty years as a hermit.

In the Mirón Park there is this viewpoint called Cuatro Vientos and next to it the monument that dedicated the city of Soria to the marriage of Machado and Leonor. It is the walking place of Machado and Leonor, who is already ill.
Enjoy the magnificent views of Soria.
